entry.title: Re: BTP Toolbox: App to Backup & Restore HDI Containers
entry.content:
Hi  @Ben 

Great Ben for showing BTP Toolbox to the SAP Community. Keep exploring and share new blogs to all 🙂

entry.title: Tcode F-90 / App with the same funtionality in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello experts!

Could you please clarify to me if in SAP Public cloud there is some app same as tcode F-90 in onpremise implementation?.
My client does not have logistic module implementation but only FI module and I´m wondering if there is any app that can make aposting vendor against fixed asset?

Thanks!

entry.title: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content:
Hi there.

I have prepared a new server to migrate our installation from WS2012+SQL2016 and SBO 10.0 FP2208 to WS2022+SQL2019 to SBO 10.0 FP2402 and then FP2405.

The installation and migration process was all correct.

Problem: SboMail and alert system. They do not work.

I have reviewed installation notes, reinstalled, SAP notes and nothing solves the problem.

Sending emails from the server from sqlmail works ok (from the transaction notification in sap database as well).

Sending emails from the B1if also works correctly.

Only from SAP it is not possible.

The same mailbox and configuration everywhere.

It can also be sent from SAP and through Outlook, except for the alert system.

Checking the operating system event viewer, I have an error saying event 62, source SBO Mail. The component causing this event is not installed on the local computer or the installation is corrupted.

What could we be missing from the configuration?

What could we be missing in the installation?

The Windows service is working without errors and the system and database configurations are correct, without errors of any kind.

entry.title: Re: CAP: Consume External Service - Part 1
entry.content:
The URL provided for the edmx didn't work for me (probably the web service changed in time)

Finally I've found the edmx V4 it at https://services.odata.org/v4/northwind/northwind.svc/$metadata

entry.title: Please let me know how to know what each component name in BPs indicate SAP module/solution name?
entry.content:
There are the following component names in Best Practices scope item.

Please let me know what each component name indicates SAP module/solution (FI/SD/MM/PP...etc).

I think a list such as  "component name" - "SAP module/Solution name".

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano and SAP Team,

My name is Hang from Vietnam. I had worked as a SAP consultant in PP module for over 10 years and now I'm working as a new ABAP developer.

I would really appreciate getting a free SAP certification because it provide me an advantage in my job.

Please kindly consider my application.

Have a nice week.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP CDC REST API Error - Phone number is not whitelisted
entry.content:
SAP Customer Data Cloud (Gigya) REST API accounts.tfa.phone.sendVerificationCode is throwing an error as given below,

{
"callId": "b0e7a2c7***********2b6eb6ab42",
"errorCode": 400030,
"errorDetails": "Phone number is not whitelisted",
"errorMessage": "Operation not allowed",
"apiVersion": 2,
"statusCode": 400,
"statusReason": "Bad Request",
"time": "2024-06-10T05:57:35.323Z"
}

The site uses extension as SMS provider (no out of the box provider user). The TFA was attempted using gigya screens.

How to whitelist the number or resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: That's good news, even though I now mainly use Eclipse for coding. Where I still need the SAP GUI is the creation/adaption of "old" dynpros/screens and that's really no fun with SAP GUI Java. Therefore, the missing graphical layout editor would be the most important feature for me. 🙂

In New learning, there's some delay for data changes.

entry.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.6 で nftablesを設定するとSAPのレスポンスが悪化する
entry.content:
SAP response slows down when nftables is configured on Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.6.
I have searched through Notes, including past cases, but have not found any hits. What logs do I need to collect to investigate the cause? If there is a relevant Note, please let me know.

entry.title: Certification
entry.content:
I am sorry for posting this here, but I don’t know where else to post this. So here goes. I am writing this post for two main reasons. This is with regard to the SAP Certification exams held in India.

  1. Based on my personal experience, I would like to warn all potential SAP certification candidates to be careful when applying for the exam in India. I attended a 4-month course and appeared for the SAP-Certified Associate-Back-end-Developer-ABAP Cloud exam on 30th May 2024. I had also studied using a sample paper after double and cross-checking all the answers from trustworthy sources including the SAP help portal and SAP community. So I was very well-versed in the questions and answers of the sample paper which was the exact same paper set by the SAP certification board. So imagine my shock when I got only 56% in the exam which I was sure to pass with a minimum percentage of 90% or more. When I asked them to show me my answer sheet and how they graded it, there was just deathly silence at their end. Still awaiting their response. Please note that the exam fees are quite expensive for many who just want to get a job or a promotion to take care of their families. I would also like to inform the candidates that the proctors are clueless on how to guide a candidate using IOS.
  2. The second important reason for posting this is to request whoever is globally responsible for SAP Certifications to please make the whole process transparent and ethical.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Where will I get the step 7 information ?
When I am checking in BTP cockpit to create the instance in BAS, Create option is disable for me.

And when I am going to create service, "SAP Business Application Studio" it's showing that this service is already subscribed in my plan.



What should I do, Please guide me.

Thanks,

Visha

entry.title: PM order print issue with 2023 upgrade
entry.content:
Previous we are using S4 HANA 2019, with Tcode IW3D (print Maintenance order) shopfloor papers used to be printed. With upgraded to 2023, system is checking for OPD setting instead of shopfloor setting. Is there any setting to change back to shopfloor papers instead of OPD? 

S4 HANA 2023

S4 Hana 2019

Regards, 

Varma

entry.title: Re: Auto Q block at storage location
entry.content:
Hi Danko,

Inspection type 08 (stock transfer inspection) is available for this in the QM module.

Note that there is configuration which defines which movement types will trigger an inspection lot when the material is activated with inspection type 08. By default movement type 311 (and 312) is one of them.

In the QM module you can create the inspection plan so this data is copied into the created inspection lot to define which quantities and inspections should be executed.

 

greetings, Herman

entry.title: Re: Space tables exist in other spaces Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey! 

If you have already checked thoroughly in all you spaces for a replication flow that would use that same table, the only recourse we had on our side was to open an OSS ticket with SAP so that they can clean some runtime artefacts in the background.

It can happen sometimes with network issue that they're not cleaned properly apparently. Also at the time we opened this ticket back in February 24 they weren't any way to clean up at customer's side unfortunately.

Best regards

entry.title: Bad sequence in luw from EWM to ERP
entry.content:
Hello,

I noticed a problem when integrating EWM queues into the ERP. it happens that the sequence of Luw is not the right one.

For example on inbound deliveries, several luws will fall into error "M7021 Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use" these luws correspond to the passage of stock from ROD to AFS.
I have to save them until the ROD reception's luw was integrated, then I restore the saved luws and everything is back to normal.

We cannot reproduce the problem, it appears approximately 5 times per month.

Is there a setting on EWM or ERP to fix this problem?

regards,

Nicolas ALBERT

entry.title: Re: New 2H 2023 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Hi Volker,

This is very nice & descriptive blog. 🙂

I have one doubt regarding 2.2.4: Enhanced options for time sheet submit dates
I'm using this feature for "Positive" time recording method & in "time recording submission configuration" I'm not assigning any submission rule & adding deviation as -2 (also I tried as +3 & by assigning submission rule). Then, I'm assigning this submission configuration to "time recording profile"

After that for employee I'm adding some entries in the time sheet but, still timesheet is not getting submitted automatically.

Could you please let me know if I'm missing any step here.

Thanks,
Dhanashree

entry.title: Re: ABAP Platform Trial 1909 Available Now
entry.content:
Dear @jand66,

you tried to pull the Release "1909_SP01" from Docker-Hub. This was replaced by a NEW release a while ago. Please check sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial - Docker Image | Docker Hub and use the pull command: "docker pull sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022"

Kind regards, Stephan

please provide more information. What is the error in detail? 

I kindly ask you to provide screenshots or log files.

Thank you for your help.

Kind regards

Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: SAP build Process Automation is not capturing SAP GUI window completely
entry.content: Hi Preeti,

entry.title: Calculating the overdue based on lead months and quantity coming from different rows
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a requirement where i have to calculate the Overdue quantity in crosstab based on some complex logic, explained below:

For the overdue calculation, formula is made up of two parts: Part A + Part B

Part A = Take the overdue of previous row and multiply it with current BOM
Note: When we are calculating the first overdue, it won't have any previous overdue, but same formula will apply, Part A will become 0, since 0 * BOM = 0.

Part B = Look at the Lead months for the previous row. Eg. if it says 4, then for previous row, add the first 4 months data (eg. this is June month, so June data + July data + august data + September data). Once done, multiply it with current BOM.

then your overdue for the current month would be equal to Part A + Part B.

@nscheaffer please help here

entry.title: Re: bods job took long time to execute
entry.content:
Hi @siba_pkd21 ,

Check the performance statistics of that dataflow from SAP Data Services Management console. It will give you an idea of where the dataflow spent much time.

Regards,

Joe

entry.title: Re: SAP build Process Automation is not capturing SAP GUI...
entry.content: Hi , first of all: which version do you have of the SAP GUI. What I know is that the 64 bits version of SAP GUI 880 is not working. You should use the 32 bits version. And second: did you declare the elements username and password? Kind regards, Jacqueline Wouters

entry.title: OAuth URL for Ariba CIG used by SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

At this moment we are in the process of implementing ALM and we need to monitor ARIBA CIG integrations; On the Ariba APIS site we already carried out the steps of creating a new application, requesting access, Ariba approved the access and we generated the oauth, now we need to know how to generate or copy the API url that we are going to use in ALM or please can you help finalize the implementation.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: OAuth URL for Ariba CIG used by SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
This topic is documented in the following KBA:
3479869 - How to find URLs used by SAP Cloud ALM for SAP Ariba Managed Gateway for Spend&Network?

In summary, all URLs relevant to monitoring SAP Ariba Managed Gateway for Spend&Network using SAP Cloud ALM can be found in the Setup for Managed Gateway for Spend&Network guide on the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal.

entry.title: How to keep desktop agent session alive with unattended mode in RDP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

How to keep desktop agent session alive always with unattended mode in RDP, after few hours if desktop agent is not in use it is getting disconnected in the RDP.

Could you please help me how I can keep the desktop Agent session alive always with unattended mode.

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: Re: BODS migration
entry.content:
@divikaus 

This is only necessary if you are using DataDirect as your ODBC driver manager.

 

Spoiler
source $LINK_DIR/DataDirect/odbc/odbc.sh

 

If you are using UnixODBC Driver manager, sourcing the $LINK_DIR/DataDirect/odbc/odbc.sh will change the path to your ODBCINI,ODBCINST environment variables breaking things.

Also unless the user is familiar with what each parameter in $LINK_DIR/conf/DSConfig.txt does, i would not encourage them to modify the file manually. 

Regards,

Joe

entry.title: Re: CPM Change Management
entry.content:
Hi

Its look like need to use below BAdI s to change this standard implementation.

/PICM/BADI_A_APPROVAL_PROCESS

/PICM/BADI_ICM_FP_PLNHDR_IMPL

Any other thought process.

Please suggest

 

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: Re: Linking Project WBS to Timesheets in the "Manage My T...
entry.content:
Hi Ganesh,

You can link the project WBS to the timesheet in the "Manage My Timesheet" app through clicking on the "3 horizontal lines" in "My Tasks" bar at the right side of the screen.

Later you will be navigated to new screen; click on "Create Tasks" and select "Unstaffed Projects". A new pop-up screen will be displayed enter the necessary details such as "Work Package", "Billing Control Category" & "Activity Type" and then save.

Your task for the associated project will display upon which the hours can be booked.

Regards,

Evans Aarons

entry.title: Re: Unable to Activate Event Mesh Webhook Subscription
entry.content: I have been able to check further and can see that the resource limit is limited to the amount that the subscription allows.

entry.title: Re: SAP build Process Automation is not capturing SAP GUI...
entry.content:

We are using SAP GUI 770. We are facing issue to declare elements username and password as full GUI screen is not getting loaded/captured.

Regards,

Siddhant

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP : Custom Entities with compositions relationship in a Fiori Elements App
entry.content:
@Ramjee_korada - I have run into another issue now related to Pagination.  My list report is unable to display more than 20 records.  On debugging, I found that the get_page_size method returns 20, and if I try to display anything more than 20 records, I get a dump with page size overrun exception.  I went through some of your other blogs and applied the pagination on the table to ensure that I do not display more than the page_size returned.  This is working fine, but I do not see any option to display the remaining records - there is no option to scroll down / go to next page.  Any help on this is greatly appreciated.

Also, is there some config where this default page size (which I see as 20) can be increased ?  

Thanks,

Vijay

entry.title: Implementing XSUAA for User Authentication in SAP CAP with HANA Database
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enthusiasts,

I am in the midst of architecting a SAP CAP-based backend coupled with SAP UI5 on the frontend. The objective is to weave in Authentication and Authorization functionalities, particularly utilizing XSUAA, while user credentials are securely stored in the HANA Cloud Database.

Imagine we have a login page. Upon entering their details and hitting the submit button, the system is designed to authenticate users against the records in the HANA Cloud Database. Successful authentication would result in the issuance of a token, which is then used to authorize subsequent API requests.

I would appreciate any insights or guidance on how to effectively implement this requirement.

Could you please help me how I can keep the desktop Agent session alive always with unattended mode.

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: Error in deployment for SAP Build Apps backend project
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am trying to redeploy SAP Build Apps backend to development after making some changes to entities. However, I am getting an error as shown in attached screenshots without any details.

Failure

Operation failed

I had deployed it to Development before without any errors its just started happening recently and just not working although I have tried to copy the project and deploy it too. 

Please advise if any way we can find more information about errors in deployment.

 

Please note I am using BUILD APPS through Partner/Standard plan to create an app. I am able to launch the app with available data using cloud functions - just getting deployment error for backend.

@Dan_Wroblewski Please help if you can thanks!

entry.title: BP error - Township has no entry
entry.content:
We are not able to do any changes in BP.

Error: Field 'Township' with short description 'Township' has been set within Customizing as a required field. There is, however, no entry in this field.

Not eble to find the Field Township in Configure Field Attributes for Each Role Category (0001-3500). Transaction: V_TB108

Where we can find Township?  Or How this issue can be resolved?

entry.title: Re: Manuals SAP PI PO Cloud Integration Data Services
entry.content:
Honestly: SAP PI/PO is coming to the end of standard support in 2027 and end of extended support in 2030. Do you really want to invest time to learn it? It has a steep learning curve and is much less documented than Cloud Integration. But if you like, you can buy the leatest SAP Press book about PI/PO. That is probably the best way to get started. 

For Cloud Integration, you find plenty of resources online, besides SAP Press books. 

E.g. the SAP Help pages, including the well written design guidelines: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/getting-started-with-integration-flow-development?locale=en-US

Best Regards, 

Philippe

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Welcome @Jan_D1 - thanks for joining. I'm part of the SAP Community team and based in Galway, Ireland. Have visited your beautiful city of Vienna in the past. Hope you get all the info you need here. Be sure to join some discussions also!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I would like to get a one-time SAP certification Exam (CER001). Thank you so much for your support.

Best Regards, 

Trinh Nguyen.

entry.title: Re: SAP DS Designer not able to execute a Job in a remote...
entry.content:
1. The Request to execute the job is sent to the Job Server port (Normally, if this port is blocked or Job Server down, when you hover your mouse on the Job Server Icon in designer you should see "Job Server not responding" on the left). When you run the job you should see "Job Server not Working!"
  2. The Job Server communicates back to the designer machine via the Notification Server Ports (To send back the logs, that you see, when the job is running). If these ports are blocked, When you hover the cursor on the Job Server icon in the designer machine you should see "Notification Server port error" message. When you run the Job you should see "Job Server not Working!"

To Conclude:
In the designer machine, make sure that the Designer communication ports (Notification Server Ports) have been set up and that inbound communication to those ports has been opened

In the Job Server machine make sure that the Job Server port has been opened and that inbound communication is allowed.

entry.title: Time Series Forecasting Model Performance Evaluation in Predictive Planning
entry.content:
Introduction

Predictive Planning relieves you of the hassle of building a time series forecasting model. What remains to be done is evaluating the model performance and checking if it meets your requirements. Predictive Planning provides everything you need for that purpose. In this blog post I guide you through the process of evaluating the performance of time series forecasting models created in Predictive Planning.

Global Performance Indicators

The global performance indicators are metrics that allow estimating the accuracy of a time series forecasting model. These performances indicators are available in the first section of the Forecast modeling report. By default, Predictive Planning shows the Expected MAPE that is considered a good, all purposes performance indicator.

Beside the Expected MAPE, Predictive Planning proposes 4 other performance indicators: Expected MAE, Expected MASE, Expected RMSE and Expected R².

You can configure what performance must be displayed and how they must be displayed by clicking the Settings button (gear shaped button) of the Global Performance Indicators visualization. In the Visualization Settings dialog you can select the performance indicators you want to display as shown below:

When you click OK, the Global Performance Indicators visualization is updated to with the selected indicators as shown below:

How to Choose a Performance Indicator?

You may wonder why you would need different performance indicator and which one you should use to evaluate the performance of your time series forecasting model.

Which performance indicator you should use depends on your business requirements. One of the first step of a forecasting project is defining the requirements in terms of forecasting performance. What is the acceptable amount of forecasting error? Is it expressed as a percentage of error or an absolute amount of error? Are large errors for a single date a specific concern? Different requirements will naturally map to different performance indicators.

In the next section we will explain each performance indicator in details, but if you don’t have time for a long read, the following summary provides guidance you can start with:

  • use MAPE if you want to be sure the performance of several time series is comparable (for instance when comparing entities of the model). If you really don’t know which performance indicator you should use then MAPE is probably a good choice. Don’t use MAPE if the time series to be forecasted contains a lot of zero values (see One Common Issue with MAPE section of this post)
  • use MAE when you want the error to be expressed in the same unit as the time series and you don’t need to compare the performance across several time series.
  • use RMSE instead of MAE if large errors are a specific concern.
  • MASE and R² are usually more useful for deeper analysis by seasoned data scientists.

I encourage you to become familiar with the different performance indicators proposed in Predictive Planning. Let’s dive into more details that will enlighten the above guidance.

MAPE

MAPE stands for Mean Absolute Percentage Error and is defined by the formula:

where Ai is the actual value and Fi is the predicted value.

MAPE represents the average percentage of error ignoring the sign of the error (so positive errors don’t compensate the negative errors). Therefore, the lower the MAPE, the better. It’s the default performance metric in Predictive Planning because of its main advantage: the MAPE is expressed as a percentage, allowing to compare the performance of different time series.

MAE

MAE stands for Mean Absolute Error and is defined by the formula:

where Ai is the actual value and Fi is the predicted value.

The MAE represents the absolute amount of error ignoring the sign of the error (so positive errors don’t compensate the negative errors).  The main advantage of the MAE is that the error is expressed directly in the same unit as the forecasted time series. If you are forecasting a quantity in USD, then a MAE of 56 can be directly interpreted as: “on average the error was of 56 dollars”. The lower the MAE the better (as it’s an amount of error).  The main MAE drawback is that it depends on the scale of the time series (is the forecasted amount large or small?) and therefore doesn’t allow comparing the performance of two time series properly. For instance, let’s assume you are forecasting the number of unit sold for product A and product B. On average each month you sell 1000 units of product A and 100 000 units of product B.  The same MAE of 500 unit would be probably considered a rather high for product A and rather low for product B.

MASE

MASE stands for Mean Absolute Scaled Error and compares the performance (based on MAE) of the evaluated time series model to a “naïve” model, rather than providing an amount of error.

MASE is defined by the formula:

where MAE is the MAE of the considered time series model and MAEnaive is the MAE of a naïve lag1 model, that is, a model that predict at date d the value of date d-1.

Values lower than 1 indicates that the evaluate model performs better than a naïve model while values higher than 1 indicate that the naïve model performs better than the evaluated model.

RMSE

RMSE stands for Root Mean Squared Error and is defined by the formula:

where Ai is the actual value and Fi is the predicted value.

RMSE is somewhat similar to MAE: it expresses an amount of error in the same unit as the forecasted time series and therefore is easy to interpret but doesn’t allow comparing performance of several time series. The difference between RMSE and MAE resides in the “square” term: it makes RMSE more sensitive to outliers (and generally speaking large errors). MAE is usually a better choice if large errors is not a specific concern.

R²

R² (R squared) represents the proportion variance of the time series that is explained by the model and is defined by the formula:

where Ai is the actual value, μ is the mean of the actual values and Fi is the predicted value.

R² also can be interpreted as a comparison between the evaluated model and a naïve model that would always predict the mean of the time series. R² must be used carefully as it requires the time series to have specific properties (stationarity) to be a reliable performance estimator.

One Common Issue with MAPE

MAPE is proposed in Predictive Planning as the default performance metric to evaluate time series forecasting models because it has several interesting qualities: it’s easy to interpret (it’s simply an amount of error expressed in percentage) and it can be compared across different models (or entities). In most cases the MAPE is a very appropriate metric to evaluate the performance of your time series forecasting models.

However, when your time series has a lot of values close to zero, you should avoid using the MAPE to evaluate the performance of your time series forecasting model. When the forecasted time series has a lot of values actual values that are close to zero, the MAPE tends to become deceptively large. In such cases your time series model seems to have a very bad performance (very high percentage of error) while it can actually be a fairly good model.

For instance, in the figure below the predictive model does a very good job at capturing the variations of the time series and is arguably very good.  Though, the Expected MAPE value is 277.23%. This is due to the fact that the time series has many actual values equal or close to zero.

This is easily explained by looking at the MAPE formula. For each point of the time series the amount of error is divided by the actual value. If the actual value is close to zero the result is skewed.

As a rule of thumb, if a time series forecasting model has an unexpectedly high MAPE, you should check if the time series to be predicted has many close to zero values. If it does, it is advised to use performance metrics other than the MAPE (MAE, MSE, RMSE).

Why are the performance indicators prefixed with “Expected”?

You have probably noticed that the performance metrics proposed in Predictive Planning are all prefixed with the term „Expected“. What does it mean?

The standard performance metrics (MAPE, MAE…) are calculated by comparing for each point of the test set the predicted value to the actual value. These metrics are an “acknowledgement” of how well the model performs on the past data but they tell you nothing about the performance you can expect when using the model to estimate future value. What Predictive Planning tries to approximate with the “expected” metrics is the performance the time series forecasting model may achieve when estimating future values.

What a MAPE of 12% tells you is that the time series model made 12% percent of error on the test data. What an Expected MAPE of 12% tells you that you can expect future predictions to be off by 12%.

How is the “expected” performance indicator calculated?

Internally the time series model generates as many predictions as requested horizon points. Each prediction corresponds to a different horizon. For each horizon, a per-horizon performance indicator is calculated. The Expected value of the performance indicator is the mean of all per-horizon performance indicator that have been calculated.

Forecast vs. Actual

On the Forecast vs. Actual visualization, you can see the predicted values along with a prediction interval. The predicted value for a given date is the best guess of the time series forecasting model, but it comes with a degree of uncertainty. The actual value will certainly not be strictly equal to the predicted value. The prediction interval tells you where the actual is likely to lie with a 95% confidence.

While the prediction interval is mainly meant to give you more details about the actual values you can expect in the future, it can also be interpreted as a visual hint about the model performance: a smaller prediction interval denotes less uncertainty about the future values and can be interpreted as a better ability for the time series model to provide precise predictions.

Evaluating a Multi Entity Time Series Forecasting Model

What we mean by “multi entity” time series forecasting model, is a time series forecasting model where multiple sub models have been created to handle the specificities of different slices of the planning model called entities. Such models are created by providing a set of dimensions in the Entity setting.

A time series forecasting model in Predictive Planning can be composed of up to 1000 entities (sub models): you cannot (and you should not) assess the performance of each entity individually.   To tackle that problem, the Overview report provides aggregated performance metrics and hints about some entities that should be specifically assessed.

Top and Bottom Entities

The first section of the Overview report provides the Top Entities and Bottom Entities list. Their purpose is to bring some entities of specific interest to your attention:

  • Bottom Entities: displays the 10 entities with the worst accuracy (sorted by decreasing Expected MAPE). It’s usually worth having a look specifically at these entities to understand why their performance is not as good as the performance of the other entities.
  • Top Entities:  displays the 10 entities with the best performance (sorted by increasing Expected MAPE).

The reason for checking the entities with a low accuracy is obvious, but why should you care about the entities with the best accuracy? A good accuracy is good news after all. Yes…most of the time. But it can also reveal hidden issues.

For instance, a 0% MAPE as shown on the screenshot below must be considered as suspiciously good. Sure, sometimes the KPI you want to forecast has a very regular behavior and it's possible to predict it perfectly. A perfect model doesn’t necessarily mean something is wrong. But it’s clearly worth checking why these entities are perfectly predicted.

More often than not, such a small Expected MAPE indicates that entities with a very small historical period (between 2 and 4 historical data points) as illustrated below:

In this specific example, we can see that the time series forecasting model is trained using only two data points. The Expected MAPE is calculated using only the very last point and is therefore way too optimistic. When the number of historical data points is very small (fewer than 5 points) it’s not possible to extrapolate realistic future values. Small data history often is the sign of emerging entities (for instance new products introduced to the market recently) or discontinued entities (for instance products that have been removed from the market).  Usually, it’s a good idea to exclude such entities from the model as emerging entities are too recent to extrapolate realistic trends and discontinued entities don’t even need to be forecasted.  If you decide to keep these entities in the model because you absolutely need a forecast, you must keep in mind that the estimated accuracy is largely overestimated.

Global Performance Indicators (Multi Entity Models)

The Overview report also provides statistic about the performance aggregated across all the entities.

For each performance indicator you can get the median, average, 3rd quartile and sum across all the entities. By default, only median and  3rd quartile are displayed. You can display the average and the sum using the settings button.

The sum is mainly provided to be used with the MAE. As explained earlier, this performance indicator is scale sensitive and therefore the amount of error will tend to be larger for larger entities. Median, average and 3rd quartile are not much relevant for MAE, as is not comparable between entities. When working with MAE as performance indicator, it’s more relevant to consider the total amount of error across all the entities (sum).

The average is provided in case it’s relevant for you but keep in mind that it can be easily skewed by extreme values (entities with a very high Expected MAPE for instance). Usually, the median is more representative of the global model performance.

Predictive Model List

When forecasting a time series, you will usually test different settings leading to different models. You need to compare these models and choose the one that brings the best performance. This is what the Predictive Models list allows you to do. The Predictive Model list is located at the bottom of the Predictive Planning user interface.

This list is customizable so you can compare the different models using the performance indicators that are the most relevant to you. To display the column selection dialog (screenshot below) click the “Select Column” button (gear shaped button in the top right corner of the list).

For the model comparison to be meaningful you must compare what’s comparable. But what does that mean exactly? First and foremost, it means comparing the performance of models that deal with the same business case. It doesn’t make sense comparing the performance of a model that forecasting travel expenses to the performance of a model forecasting the sales of a product.
From a practical standpoint this means that the predictive models within the same predictive scenario should all correspond to different experiment to answer the same business question. You should always have one distinct predictive scenario per predictive question and refrain from mixing unrelated models into the same predictive scenario.

Conclusion

In this blog post you learned how to use the Predictive Planning user interface to assess the accuracy of your time series forecasting models.

Do you want to learn more on Predictive Planning?

  • Find more blog posts about Predictive Planning and Smart Predict.
  • Explore our learning track.
  • Go hands-on and experience SAP Analytics Cloud by yourself.

entry.title: Re: How to refresh or reload a page or explicitly call on...
entry.content: @Jitendra_Kansal  @bill_froelich Can you please help me over here.

entry.title: Re: Working with SQL query generator variables
entry.content:
Hello, 
I'm working with Hana, I adapted the sintax as @giacomozzi  wrote before, and it works if I try to select the value saved in the variable, but, If I try to use the variable for a stored procedures it not works.

That's what tried:

/*SELECT FROM OSRT P0*/
declare StartDate date;
StartDate := /* P0.FromDate */ [%0];

/*SELECT FROM OSRT P0*/
declare EndDate date;
EndDate := /* P0.ToDate */ [%1];

 If I try to do:

Select StartDate, EndDate, * from OINV

It works, I can see all the invoices and in the first two columns the value stored in the variables

But if I try:

CALL MyStored(StartDate, EndDate)

It does not work, no error is showed, but neither the result.
Of course if I try to call the stored, with the dates instead of the variables, it works and I got the result.

Why this?

BR

Diego

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT PROGRAM with export-import internal tables issue
entry.content:
As this is a NO-DISPLAY parameter, you could give it the same definition than your internal table (type table of)

  • You could also EXPORT data in a permanent medium such as DATABASE or SHARED MEMORY/BUFFER

entry.title: Re: storage location field greyed out for the delivery item
entry.content: Hi, the issue got resolved with the SAP Note 3054191 - storage location and delivery quantity cannot be changed

entry.title: Re: BODS migration
entry.content:
Hello @jmuiruri ,

Thanks for the feedback. This blog was only in the context of a migration from Oracle to HANA database for BoDS with consistent patch and functionality throughout I.e re-using the Existing DSConfig file.

For HaNA as underlying database you have to use UNIXodbc, but souring odbc.ini from DataDirect does not impact that connectivity - and this is only a suggestion in the blog - not a requirement.

Regards,

kaustubh.

entry.title: Settlement Management Accrual Reversal RCA2 Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

when i create a partial settlement it is somehow fetching a greater amount for accrual reversal for all the items. any idea what could be causing this. in the Business vol config all the CDS views are maintained for accrual reversal.

entry.title: Date of manufacturing entry at a time of Good Receipt in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I complete an order in the process industry and create a Goods Receipt for that order, I am unable to see any details related to the manufacturing date of the order; instead, I only see the posting date.

 

Upon checking the integration message dashboard, I found a manufacturing date available. Additionally, I need to clarify whether this manufacturing date is the material creation date or the actual manufacturing date.

We have already maintained SLED data in ERP, so the manufacturing date is crucial.

(Note: When converting the manufacturing date to UTC date, it showed as 15/02/2024, which coincides with my material creation date.)

When I check in ERP in Material Movement, I cannot see the Date of Manufacturing.

 

Please point out if I am missing any configuration issues related to the manufacturing date in creating Goods Receipts in DM or in ERP.


Regards,
Kartik Singhal

entry.title: How to hide Custom field in Billing Document item based on condition in SAP S/4 Hana Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

How to hide custom fields in Billing Document Item based on Condition? Same requirement i had in Sale order screen and achieved it through BADI. But i don't see similar BADI released for billing document to set properties of custom fields to Invisible / mandatory / read-only?

It would be grateful if you could help me in hiding custom fields in billing document item level.

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: Robosapiens and SAPGUI Library in Robot Framework: At...
entry.content: Hi @stefan_schnell, as mentioned in the comment already .VBS File executed for the following code successfully. session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subSCREEN_1:SAPLIQS0:1060/txtVIQMEL-QMTXT").text = "TestAutomation". I am using SAP 750 Version. Not sure whats the issue in SapGUiLibrary. Its not issue with Robosapiens. I am trying to use Input Text  wnd[0]/usr/subSCREEN_1:SAPLIQS0:1060/txtVIQMEL-QMTXT CustomerNotification which is part of SAPGUilibrary's Keyword. The text field which I am trying to enter the value does not have Label for it so couldn't use Robosapiens.

Looking to understand if we are able to condense the amount of MRP controllers within the system and assign correct tasks to the correct Controller.

Are the planner codes different and have differing responsibilities?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Custom fields flow from Sale order to Purchase Requisition and Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

I have created few custom fields in Sale order item level, and i wanted those to flow to Purchase requisition and Purchase order in SAP S4HANA Public Edition.

I checked in Business Scenarios Tab while creating custom fields but unable to find PR and PO to enable.

It would be grateful if you could help me.

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori adoption enabled by Security Role Design
entry.content:
Dear @RaminS ,

Thank you for the comment and the query. 

Organizations can approach it in various ways, and there is no standard method for performing such a migration. 

When defining the approach, certain factors should be taken into consideration, such as,

  1. Current security role design and its readiness to support Fiori catalog/spaces/group based design.
  2. Fiori deployment - embedded or other possible methods.
  3. Fiori adoption - big bang vs. phase wise adoption.
  4. Fiori design principles - Fiori catalogs resembling a Business role or aligned with business tasks in BPML
  5. Ambition to make the Fiori Launchpad the sole entry point into the system, or to maintain SAP GUI as an option for business users.

 

Lessons learnt :

  • Fiori apps shouldn't be considered 1:1 replacement for transaction codes. They are often superset or subset of a transaction code, so does the associated authorization objects. Additional review needed when referring current GUI roles to update authorizations data in Fiori role.
  • Review of relevance of semantic actions and target mappings when Fiori catalog is designed referring standard business or technical catalogs.
  • If the existing security role's architecture/design doesn't support intended UX on Fiori launchpad, that becomes a necessary change for Fiori adoption.
  • SU24 maintenance should be made a priority when adopting Fiori apps.

entry.title: Re: CDS based Analytical oData for Fiori
entry.content: We're experiencing similar difficulties with analytical services for Analytical List Pages. SAP's documentation is insufficient.

entry.title: PO with multi Down/Advance payment
entry.content:
PO with multi Down/Advance payment

Dears,
I'm looking to how implement more than one Down/Advance payment when I create the PO.
Ex: I agreed with a supplier to pay the PO amount (1000$) as the following:


500$ once PO is approved.
300$ after 30 days
200$ after deliver all goods.

The system has only one down payment. 

How can I implement this in PO (payment processing).

entry.title: Create an automatic profile generator with ABAP
entry.content:
Hello , 
I need to create a programme in the sap system. Here is the detail: 

The programme should process the user profiles automatically.
it fetches all used transactions and checks which transaction was used in the last 3 months.
The programme takes these transactions of the user and deletes the unused transactions. All this information is stored in a table. Based on this information, the transactions used are summarised in a profile. This profile is assigned directly to the user. At the end, the user receives a message that they have received a new profile.
Do you have any Idea , how to do that ? 

Thanks

Best, 
Sophia

entry.title: Fiori UI5 "Failed to invoke: .. Unknown operation:" in OData V4
entry.content:
hello Community, 

I have the problem with UI5 development that I cannot call our action.

The error appears in the console: "Failed to invoke /PostGoodsMvT(...) - Error: Unknown operation: /PostGoodsMvT(...)"

I have already tested all the different methods from the OData Operations website OData Operations - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK (ondemand.com), but I always get the same error.

The metadata was created by the ‘SAP Fiori Application generator’ in Visual Studio Code. I have selected ‘Custom Page’.

Displaying data via CDS-views from the OData V4 service works without problems.

Only the action call does not work. My ABAP colleague set up the OData V4 according to the RAP instructions. I have tested another OData V4 service which works with a different app and I get the same error.

My Code: (For this example, I have kept the parameters static without input.)
Call Action:

View:

Metadata:

Error:


Best regards,

Lukas

Marc

entry.title: Re: Guidelines for API Journal Entry – Post
entry.content:
@Wesley 

Hello,
since the tax amounts needs to be calculated manually, ist here there any API or service class that calculates the tax amounts on document level as it would be needed for the posting. 
Lets assume I habe a document with about 300 items(cross values), some with tax codes ( V1 V2 V5 V0) some without. I need to calculate the correct amounts first the net amounts for each cross value and then the tax values, but theses shall not be calculated on line item level, but on document level.

Is there any calculation help that can be used? Or can the API calculate this on it's own now (current Version 2023 FSR1).

Kind regards 
Markus

“How HR Increases Employee Engagement with SAP’s Business Technology Platform” Global Series

Join us for a three-part webinar series in which SAP experts will explain how SAP SuccessFactors customers can take advantage of the innovations offered by the Business Technology Platform specifically for HR practitioners.

Part 1: The key to a transformation is an effective approach to integration that can unlock the potential for people data. This session will illustrate why the strategy for HR data & process integration should be owned & executed by HR and how SAP’s Business Technology Platform can assist.

11:00 – 12:00 EDT

17:00 – 18.00 CEST

sharon.o-neill@sap.com and samanta.ferreri@sap.com

entry.title: APP 'Manage Material Valuations' shows inventory value zero for products valuated at MAP
entry.content:
Hi,

The app 'Manage Material Valuations' shows inventory value zero for products valued at moving average price although there's a total stock value and stock quantity. Why is the inventory value shown as zero?

You would expect the inventory price to be total stock value / stock quantity in this case.

Thanks in advance for your support,
Jonathan

Regards

Gaurav Kumar

entry.title: Document Management Service questions
entry.content:
Hello,

I need to implement Document Management Service into our Application but are having hard times understanding what we really need.

Our application generates PDF reports and I'd like to upload them somewhere, where SAP users can View and Download them.

Is this service what I need?

Also, reading the documentation

What is the difference between:

  • Integration Option

  • Application Option

  • Repository Option

?

 

We already have

ObjectStore  implemented and up and running on AWS S3

 

is this the repository?

entry.title: How to call public cloud SAP API from embedded steampunk?
entry.content:
After days of reading documentation, blogs and tutorials... I'm still no step further in what I was expecting to be a pretty straight-forward scenario. I must be missing something obvious, but what?

On our S/4HANA Public Edition development system, I have created an application job to automatically create credit memos. Works like a charm, up until the moment that I actually need to create the credit memo. Of course we want to use the available public API to do so.

There is a default communication arrangement for this API (SAP_COM_0158), which has INBOUND services linked. Makes sense, since I want to call this service to create the credit memo in SAP. That sounds indeed like an inbound interface. It should not matter whether I call it from an actual external system (like BTP) or in this case from the embedded ABAP environment. As far as S/4 is concerned, it is inbound.

This tutorial seems to come closest to what I want to achieve:
Implement an Outbound Service Call in SAP BTP, ABAP environment for an OData Service via Service Consumption Model 

Several components are required to make this work. We have a communication scenario ZCS_CREDIT_MEMO, in which I point to outbound service ZOBS_CREDIT_MEMO_REST. Then there is the consumption model ZCM_CREDIT_MEMO, based on the EDMX file from the business hub. It provides me with example code.

To get the destination, static method CREATE_BY_COMM_ARRANGEMENT is being called. Following the tutorial, I enter the communication scenario and the outbound service. This results in an exception. Apparently not the values SAP is expecting. I've tried several combinations of objects, so far nothing works.

DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
                                             comm_scenario  = 'ZCS_CREDIT_MEMO'
                                             service_id     = 'ZOBS_CREDIT_MEMO_REST' ).

In the debugger I can see the query to retrieve the communication arrangements uses table aps_ca_d_root as a basis. Unfortunately, this being public cloud, I cannot view the contents of that table to see what is actually there. That would be helpful in finding what I should provide the method.

In case I enter the communication arrangement as 'comm_scenario', the system does find the communication arrangement itself, but then filters on available outbound services. Which it does not have, since it's meant for inbound. Once again, no result, which results in the exception.

This has to be incredibly basic. What step am I missing here? Thanks in advance for the help!

Both queries are on the same system and when i choose the hierachy mentioned in the Screenshot, all my widgets are not migrating properly - the error in Screenshot 2 occurs. 

In other How-tos and Blogs on the internet i found, one is able to match Measure by measure (Screenshot 3 ), somehow i dont have this option.

Maybe i am missing something.

Mirko

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi @Ruthiel  It seems you are the step 7, not 9.

It is most likely that the password stored in the key is incorrect, so I would suggest you try running the SQL statement using `hdbsql` but with host and user name instead of the user key, like:

 

~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -u DBAdmin -n 236b8dce-adfc-404f-93e9-ca01e10ddab1.hana.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com: 443 -jA "SELECT Current_User FROM DUMMY"

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration - Message search made easy with custom headers
entry.content:
Hi, I have a requirment to modify this value during run time of the Iflow and it should only store the latest assigned value.

 

Ex : I have the above scrpit at the begining of the flow and its storing the value as EMPID=2044298 and at the end of the iflow there was a logic and its modfiied the value as EMPID=00004.

Currenlty what i can see is while using the API i am able to see two entry into the CustomHeaderAPI and i can see both the values. however i only requred to see the second (EMPID=00004.)value 🙂

entry.title: SQL Database to datasphere vis DP connetor
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am trying to connect to SQL server through DP agent. I am getting this error:

Error

Connection "SA**** couldn’t be established. - Data flows can’t be used because of errors in the connection. - Replication flows are not supported. - Remote tables can’t be used because of errors in the connection.


Data Flows: Cause: Open request could not be performed due to error: Timeout expired. select failed: 2
Invalid attribute in connection string: DriverUnicodeType.
Remote Tables: Unable to connect remote source: Adapter validation failed. [DWC_*******]Failed to establish JDBC connection. Error: Fehler bei der Anmeldung für den Benutzer "datasphere". ClientConnectionId:**************, Code: 5921, SQL State: HY000

Could Someone help me out with this problem. Thank you

 

Microsoft SQL Server SAP Datasphere #dPAgent

entry.title: Re: Build Application using Python - SAP UI5 & Deploy to SAP BTP
entry.content: can you pls share full steps on how to deploy python code running locally to btp?

entry.title: Re: Can not link second e-mail address to my Universal ID
entry.content: Hello AISIA, Kindly create a case under component XX-SER-UID, and provide your UID account, new email address and issue relevant screen shot. The UID support team will check the issue and update you via the case. Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI Now Supports Custom Certificate Authorities on AWS
entry.content:
Hi Shaun,

Yes, the new solution SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI uses a cloud service to issue and provision the X.509 certificates. With the new solution you can either use the SAP-managed Cloud CA (see also the release blog here) or integrate with your custom CA running on AWS (as explained in this blog).

You no longer need to operate an on-premise server running on an SAP NetWeaver Application Server Java.

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: SAP GTS upgrade from V11 to E4H
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are upgrading from V11 to GTS E4H. Can anyone please help me from functional side what are the thing I need to consider from configuration and if any other stuff ?

Appreciate if you can confirm at the earliest

Thanks

Ramesh

entry.title: VIP Program - Create workforce plans in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning (Part 3 - APJ/EMEA)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please REGISTER HERE in order to receive the event invite and login details. 

“How HR Increases Employee Engagement with SAP Business Technology Platform” Global Series

Join us for a three-part webinar series in which SAP experts will explain how SAP SuccessFactors customers can take advantage of the innovations offered by the Business Technology Platform specifically for HR practitioners.

Part 3: HR leaders are navigating an increasingly complex market for talent, from optimizing a hybrid (office/home) workforce to engaging with contingent labor and reskilling employees. This session will illustrate how building workforce plans in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning can help your organization forecast talent trends, help HR build a future-ready workforce, and gain the flexibility and power to meet your organization’s needs.

APJ/EMEA – Thursday 26th September 2024  

09:00 – 10:00 CEST 

12:30 – 13:30 IST 

15:00 – 16:00 SGT 

17:00 – 18:00 AEST 

REGISTER HERE!

 

If you have any questions, please get in touch with the VIP Program Managers: 

sharon.o-neill@sap.com and samanta.ferreri@sap.com
